description_long: This cowshed features a rectangular structure with a pitched, red-tiled gable roof and whitewashed wooden walls adorned with subtle floral patterns. The main facade includes several rectangular openings, likely for ventilation or access, and a side extension that appears to function as a sheltered area or storage. The left side reveals an open section with a suspended blue bucket, possibly for feeding or watering livestock. The right side shows a partially enclosed area with additional access points and a covered entry. The overall design is utilitarian yet decorative, blending traditional rural elements with practical features for livestock housing. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground, with visible slats and open sections for airflow.
description: A white wooden cowshed with a red-tiled gable roof, floral patterns, and multiple access points, including a side extension and open sections for ventilation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with rectangular openings, red-tiled roof, and a side extension with a blue bucket.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the open section with the suspended blue bucket and visible interior shelving.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, red-tiled roof, and symmetrical side extensions.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the covered entry, partially enclosed area, and additional access points.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, painted, with rectangular cutout
attribute_window_presence: multiple rectangular windows on front and side, simple style
